analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-281

POL-281.title: IMPLEMENT
POL-281.body:
## Objective

Implement the [POL-277](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-277/fix-fail-packet-generation-when-impl-child-has-empty-allowed-scope-or) through [POL-280](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-280/fix-parent-loop-must-validate-commit-evidence-even-when-worker-wrote) hardening cluster so Polaris cannot dispatch empty or non-actionable worker packets and cannot produce delivery PRs from fake worker completions.

## Context

This issue is the implementation parent for the PR 72 failure diagnosis. PR 72 showed that Polaris could dispatch children with empty scope, empty validation, placeholder goals, and no actionable issue body. Workers then self-reported completion with empty commits, and finalize opened a PR even though no implementation evidence existed.

The diagnosis found three compounding failures:

1. Packet generation allowed empty or placeholder child metadata.
2. Worker adapters could complete or report failure without proving real in-scope work.
3. Finalize trusted runtime state without verifying non-artifact commit evidence, result artifacts, and validation output.

Polaris must treat empty instructions as a preflight failure, not as runnable work.

## Goal

Make the PR 72 failure mode impossible by enforcing actionable packet requirements, validating worker completion evidence, and rejecting finalize flows that lack non-artifact implementation proof.

## Scope

* [POL-277](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-277/fix-fail-packet-generation-when-impl-child-has-empty-allowed-scope-or): fail packet generation or parent preflight when a child has no body, no allowed scope, or no validation commands.
* [POL-278](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-278/fix-extend-empty-scope-guard-from-agent-subtask-adapter-to-terminal): extend empty-scope and actionable-packet guards to the terminal-cli adapter path.
* [POL-279](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-279/fix-polaris-finalize-must-verify-non-artifact-commit-diff-per-child): make finalize reject delivery when children have no non-artifact commit evidence or missing result evidence.
* [POL-280](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-280/fix-parent-loop-must-validate-commit-evidence-even-when-worker-wrote): make parent loop validate worker self-completion against commit evidence and sealed result state before checkpointing completion.
* `src/loop/parent.ts`
* `src/loop/run-preflight.ts`
* `src/loop/run-bootstrap.ts`
* `src/loop/checkpoint.ts`
* `src/loop/worker-packet.ts`
* `src/loop/worker-prompt.ts`
* `src/loop/adapters/agent-subtask.ts`
* `src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.ts`
* `src/loop/adapters/types.ts`
* `src/finalize/**`
* `src/cluster-state/**`
* Tests covering loop parent, packet generation, adapters, finalize, and cluster-state reconciliation

## Acceptance Criteria

* [POL-281](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-281/implement) packet generation includes this issue body in the worker packet and prompt.
* A child with an empty or whitespace-only body fails before dispatch state is mutated.
* A child with empty allowed scope fails before dispatch or adapter execution.
* A child with empty validation commands fails unless explicitly marked as no-validation-required with a reason.
* The agent-subtask path does not attempt to read a sealed result file when no native dispatcher launched a worker.
* The terminal-cli path enforces the same actionable-packet requirements.
* Parent loop does not checkpoint self-reported worker completion without sealed result and commit or result evidence.
* Finalize rejects a cluster where completed children have empty commits, no non-artifact changes, missing result artifacts, or inconsistent cluster-state evidence.
* Tests cover the PR 72 failure mode and prove it cannot produce another empty delivery PR.
* Existing tests continue to pass.

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`
* `npx vitest run src/loop src/finalize src/cluster-state`
* `npm run polaris -- loop status`

## Ordering

Implement in child order from [POL-277](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-277/fix-fail-packet-generation-when-impl-child-has-empty-allowed-scope-or) through [POL-280](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-280/fix-parent-loop-must-validate-commit-evidence-even-when-worker-wrote). Packet-generation gating is first, adapter and parent-loop hardening follow, and finalize verification closes the cluster. This issue should produce implementation changes, not only analysis.

## Non-goals

* Do not manually create sealed result files to bypass adapter failures.
* Do not weaken the new packet or body gates to make tests pass.
* Do not allow provider fallback or external routing changes in this issue unless required to keep dispatch safe.
* Do not treat runtime scratch files as implementation evidence.

POL-280.title: FIX: Parent loop must validate commit evidence even when worker wrote its own state completion
POL-280.body:
## Objective

Make the parent loop verify commit evidence even when a worker writes its own completion into runtime state.

## Context

In PR 72, workers wrote their own completion entries into `current-state-pol-268-reset.json`. `parent.ts` detects this via `workerWroteCompletion`.

```typescript
const workerWroteCompletion =
  reloadedStateValid &&
  (state.completed_children.includes(nextChild) ||
    state.context_budget.children_completed > childrenCompletedBeforeDispatch);
```

When `workerWroteCompletion === true`, the parent skips the dispatch boundary check and does not perform commit evidence validation. This allowed 15 children to self-report completion without verification.

The existing commit evidence check only applies when `workerStatus === 'done'`, so a worker that already wrote completion into the state file bypasses the normal validation path.

## Goal

Require the parent loop to validate self-reported worker completion before accepting it.

When `workerWroteCompletion === true`, the parent must still:

1. Read the child result file.
2. Extract the `commit` field.
3. Verify the commit hash is non-empty and exists in git.
4. Verify `git show <hash> --stat` contains at least one non-artifact file using the same rule as finalize.

If verification fails, the parent must halt with `worker-error` instead of silently accepting completion.

## Scope

* `src/loop/parent.ts` — `workerWroteCompletion` path
* `src/loop/parent.test.ts` — new test cases

## Acceptance Criteria

* Worker self-completes with empty commit and parent halts with `worker-error`.
* Worker self-completes with valid commit and real file changes and parent accepts.
* Existing tests continue to pass.

## Validation

* `npm test -- --grep "workerWroteCompletion|parent.*completion|self.*complete"`
* `npm run build`

## Ordering

Parallel with [POL-279](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-279/fix-polaris-finalize-must-verify-non-artifact-commit-diff-per-child). Finalize and the parent loop must both close this gap because they operate at different lifecycle points.

## Non-goals

* Do not relax completion checks for self-reported worker state.
* Do not accept artifact-only or empty-commit completion as sufficient evidence.
* Do not change unrelated worker lifecycle behavior outside the self-completion validation path.

POL-279.title: FIX: polaris finalize must verify non-artifact commit diff per child before creating PR
POL-279.body:
## Objective

Make `polaris finalize` verify non-artifact commit evidence for each completed child before opening or updating a delivery PR.

## Context

`polaris finalize` for the PR 72 run of [POL-268](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-268/implement-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) created PR 72 with 16 completed children, even though every recorded commit had zero changed files. Finalize read `current-state-pol-268-reset.json`, saw `completed_children` populated and `status: cluster-complete`, and created the PR without inspecting the actual git diff.

At the same time, `cluster-state.json` showed all children as `dispatched` with empty `result_pointers`, `commits`, and `validation_results`. Finalize did not cross-check that contradiction.

## Goal

Require finalize to verify that each child in `completed_children` has real implementation evidence before delivery.

Each child must satisfy all of the following:

1. A commit hash is recorded in cluster state or the result file.
2. `git show <hash> --stat` returns at least one file that is not an artifact path such as `.polaris/clusters/`, `.taskchain_artifacts/`, or `.polaris/runs/`.
3. Validation commands ran and passed, or the packet carries an explicit `validation_waiver`.

If a child fails any check, finalize must halt and identify the failing child.

Artifact-only children are allowed only when the packet explicitly sets `artifact_only: true` from foreman analysis time.

## Scope

* `src/cli/` — `polaris finalize` command
* `src/loop/` — finalize support logic if applicable

## Acceptance Criteria

* Finalize with all-empty-commit children exits non-zero and lists each failing child.
* Finalize with one artifact-only child carrying `artifact_only: true` passes for that child.
* Finalize with a real implementation child with files changed and validation passed continues normally.
* New test covers the artifact-only rejection path.

## Validation

* `npm test -- --grep "finalize|artifact.*only"`
* `npm run build`

## Ordering

Implement alongside or after [POL-277](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-277/fix-fail-packet-generation-when-impl-child-has-empty-allowed-scope-or). This is defense in depth for packets that should have been rejected earlier.

## Non-goals

* Do not trust runtime state alone when delivery evidence is inconsistent.
* Do not treat artifact-only changes as implementation evidence unless the packet explicitly authorizes artifact-only completion.
* Do not broaden finalize scope beyond child evidence verification for this issue.

POL-278.title: FIX: Extend empty-scope guard from agent-subtask adapter to terminal-cli adapter
POL-278.body:
## Objective

Extend the empty-scope guard to the terminal-cli adapter so implementation packets with no allowed scope are rejected regardless of provider.

## Context

`src/loop/adapters/agent-subtask.ts` already contains an empty-scope guard for implementation packets.

```typescript
if (isWorkerPacket(packet) && packet.worker_role === 'impl') {
  const allowed = Array.isArray(packet.instructions?.allowed_scope) ? packet.instructions.allowed_scope : [];
  if (allowed.length === 0) {
    // returns blocked result, exit_code: 1
  }
}
```

The `terminal-cli` adapter used for Copilot workers in PR 72 has no equivalent guard. All 15 Copilot-dispatched children in PR 72 passed through terminal-cli with empty-scope packets and completed almost immediately.

## Goal

Ensure terminal-cli enforces the same actionable-packet requirement as agent-subtask.

Add the empty-scope guard to terminal-cli, and optionally extract the guard into a shared `validateWorkerPacket()` helper so current and future adapters use the same validation path.

## Scope

* `src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.ts` — add empty-scope guard
* `src/loop/adapters/agent-subtask.ts` — optionally extract shared validator
* `src/loop/adapters/` — optional shared validator module

## Acceptance Criteria

* Terminal-cli returns a blocked result when `allowed_scope` is empty for an implementation packet.
* Agent-subtask retains existing guard behavior.
* If a shared validator is extracted, both adapters delegate to it.
* A new adapter test proves empty-scope implementation packets via terminal-cli are blocked with `exit_code: 1`.

## Validation

* `npm test -- --grep "terminal-cli|adapter.*scope"`
* `npm run build`

## Ordering

Blocked conceptually by [POL-277](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-277/fix-fail-packet-generation-when-impl-child-has-empty-allowed-scope-or). Packet generation should stop empty-scope packets earlier, and this issue adds adapter-level defense in depth.

## Non-goals

* Do not rely on provider-specific behavior to enforce implementation packet validity.
* Do not remove or weaken the existing agent-subtask guard.
* Do not expand this issue into unrelated adapter refactors.

POL-277.title: FIX: Fail packet generation when impl child has empty allowed_scope or placeholder goal
POL-277.body:
## Objective

Fail packet generation for implementation children when the issue body does not produce actionable scope, goal, or validation instructions.

## Context

PR 72 for [POL-268](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-268/implement-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) produced 16 empty commits across 16 children. Every packet carried `allowed_scope: []`, `validation_commands: []`, and a placeholder `primary_goal`. The child issues did not provide Goal, Scope, Acceptance Criteria, or Validation content that could be extracted into a runnable worker packet.

Workers received non-actionable packets and completed almost immediately with zero files changed.

The packet generator currently emits packets from issue metadata even when the issue body contains no usable implementation scope.

## Goal

Add a hard gate in packet generation or the dispatch path that refuses to emit an implementation worker packet when any of the following are true:

1. `allowed_scope` is empty.
2. `primary_goal` is a placeholder.
3. `validation_commands` is empty and `validation_waiver` is absent.

## Scope

* `src/loop/` — packet generation and dispatch path
* `src/cli/` — dispatch command if the gate belongs there

## Acceptance Criteria

* `npm run polaris -- loop dispatch` exits non-zero and emits `packet-generation-failed` telemetry when scope is empty.
* The error message names the missing field and the child issue ID.
* Existing tests for well-formed packets continue to pass.
* A new test proves dispatch fails clearly when an issue has no usable scope body.

## Validation

* `npm test -- --grep "packet.*empty.*scope|empty.*allowed_scope"`
* `npm run build`

## Ordering

Highest-priority fix. All other guardrails are defense in depth against a packet that should never have been generated.

## Non-goals

* Do not allow placeholder goals, empty scope, or empty validation to pass through packet generation.
* Do not defer this validation exclusively to adapter or finalize layers.
* Do not change unrelated dispatch behavior outside actionable-packet gating.

POL-268.title: IMPLEMENT: Polaris init and repository adoption workflow
